comments.body: Plan-review PASSED round 3 (signed) after 2 remediations. Impl commit 068ba1842. The gate correctly BLOCKED the original Pass-1-injection design (would break the pinned test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam + ADR 0008 Inv.1). Pivoted to the sanctioned post-Pass-1 recall step: sidecar.prior_concerns + run_pass1 adds missed prior findings (by norm_id) as _recall candidates through the UNCHANGED Pass-2 backstop; finder byte-unchanged. Fixed vague AC4 (concrete pytest cmd running the independence test too), lowercase decision filter, named constants, best-effort fallback. 5 residual advisories triaged: _recall is observability-only (underscore-internal like _too_big); best-effort covers reader-error+no-sidecar; recall-lift is #3's offline job. Tests: test_pass1_recall.py (9) + independence suite green.

compiled_state.description:
# Plan-review Pass-1 finding-memory (recall fix)

## Why
Verdict-flip on identical code is a RECALL problem: in flipping same-material review pairs the decisive
would-block findings had validity ~0.99 and 14/17 flips were because the other run simply MISSED the valid
finding. Improving finder recall closes the gap; tightening thresholds cannot.

## What (independence-preserving: a POST-Pass-1 recall step, NOT a Pass-1 finder injection)
Re-surface a ticket's prior-review findings that the fresh Pass-1 finder MISSED, then let the UNCHANGED
Pass-2 verifier decide whether they still hold — so a genuinely-resolved concern is dropped, not re-blocked.

- LOAD: read the most-recent REVIEW_RESULT sidecar via the EXISTING `sidecar.latest_review_result(ticket_id)`
  reader; take prior findings that scored NEAR/ABOVE the bar. Concrete rule (named module constants, not bare
  literals; config-tunable later): `priority >= RECALL_MIN_PRIORITY` (=0.5) AND `decision in {"block",
  "advisory"}` — the LOWERCASE decision strings pass3_decide emits, excluding "dropped"/"indeterminate" —
  capped to the top `RECALL_CAP` (=12) by priority to bound cost. Best-effort: any failure in the reader
  (missing/garbled sidecar, I/O error) degrades to an EMPTY concern list — recall is then a no-op and NEVER
  fails the review (mirrors the sidecar's observability-only posture).
- RECALL (strictly AFTER Pass-1): compute the fresh findings' `norm_id` set using the EXISTING
  `sidecar.norm_id(finding)` (no new fingerprinting); for each prior concern whose `norm_id` is NOT in that
  set (the fresh finder MISSED it), add it as a RECALL CANDIDATE finding (its prior
  `finding`/`suggested_fix`/`criteria`/`location`, tagged `_recall: true`). Dedup candidates by `norm_id`.
- VERIFY (unchanged): recall candidates flow through the SAME Pass-2 verifier + Pass-3 decision as any
  finding — each judged INDEPENDENTLY against the CURRENT plan. A concern the current plan resolved fails
  validity (< 0.5) and is DROPPED by Pass-2, so a stale recalled finding can NEVER block on memory alone.

## Structural-independence invariant (explicitly preserved — the reason for the post-Pass-1 design)
The codebase enforces finder independence "by construction, not by prompt assertion": the pinned test
`test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam` (tests/unit/test_plan_review_novelty.py) asserts the
Pass-1 finder / `verify_instructions` NEVER carry prior findings — prior findings reach ONLY the Pass-2
novelty seam (`verify.prior_findings_block`). ADR 0008 Invariant 1 is the same rule.
This design does NOT inject prior findings into the Pass-1 finder: the finder runs byte-unchanged, so
`test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam` and ADR 0008 Invariant 1 stay green. Prior findings enter
ONLY as post-Pass-1 recall CANDIDATES that are verified on their own merits (never merged into the finder's
context; never blocking without independent Pass-2 confirmation). A NEW test pins that the finder
instructions remain prior-free while recall candidates are added strictly after Pass-1.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` — a `prior_concerns(ticket_id, repo_root)` reader that reuses
  `latest_review_result` + `norm_id` (no new fingerprinting algorithm) and the `RECALL_MIN_PRIORITY`/
  `RECALL_CAP` constants; best-effort (never raises).
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py` (`run_pass1`) — the post-Pass-1 recall step (the fresh finder is
  unchanged; `ctx` already carries `ticket_id` + `repo_root`). Cost is the recalled candidates' Pass-2
  verification tokens, NOT an extra finder call.
- `tests/unit/` — recall + resolved-drop + independence-preserved tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prior-review findings (filtered `priority >= RECALL_MIN_PRIORITY` AND `decision in {"block",
      "advisory"}`, capped to `RECALL_CAP`) that the fresh Pass-1 finder MISSED (by `norm_id`) are
      re-surfaced as recall candidates AFTER Pass-1; they are NOT injected into the Pass-1 finder context and
      are deduped by `norm_id`. A reader failure degrades to a no-op (never fails the review).
- [ ] The Pass-1 finder is byte-unchanged: the pinned `test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam`
      still passes, and a NEW test asserts `run_pass1`'s finder instructions never contain prior-finding text
      (independence by construction preserved; ADR 0008 Invariant 1 upheld).
- [ ] No additional LLM FINDER call beyond the existing Pass-1. Recall candidates DO incur their own Pass-2
      verification (that is the FP backstop), bounded by the `RECALL_CAP` (<=12) cap; there is no extra
      finder pass and no new prompt.
- [ ] Proving command `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_pass1_recall.py tests/unit/test_plan_review_novelty.py -v`
      (all green — the second file runs the pinned independence test), covering: (a) a prior finding the
      fresh run MISSED is re-surfaced as a recall candidate; (b) a prior finding the fresh run ALSO found is
      NOT double-surfaced (norm_id dedup); (c) a recalled candidate whose current-plan verification FAILS
      validity is DROPPED by Pass-2, not re-blocked; (d) with no prior sidecar, recall is a no-op. Recall-lift
      on same-`material_fingerprint` pairs is measured OFFLINE by the calibration child
      (raptorial-galloping-dragon), not gated here.
compiled_state.title: Plan-review Pass-1 finding-memory (recall fix)
